source.title: PDD Take Rate Analysis and Peer Comparison

extractions.claim: PDD's blended take rate is approximately 4-5%, but this is misleading because it blends Pinduoduo (net) and Temu (gross) revenue recognition models
extractions.evidence: Analyst estimates of PDD's take rate: ~4% in 2022, projected to reach ~5% by 2025. Alibaba's marketplace take rate: ~5%. However, PDD's reported take rate is NOT directly comparable because: (1) Pinduoduo domestic revenue is NET (commission + ad fees / GMV), (2) Temu revenue includes GROSS merchandise value for fully-managed orders. A proper take rate should be calculated separately: Pinduoduo domestic take rate ~3-4% (marketplace fees on ~$500B+ GMV), Temu effective take rate is distorted by gross recognition.

verification.verifier_notes: PDD does not disclose GMV, making precise take rate calculation impossible. Estimates from analyst reports (NexGen Investors, CMBI). The blended take rate is only meaningful for trend analysis, not cross-platform comparison.

extractions.claim: Pinduoduo's domestic take rate has been rising steadily, from ~3% to an estimated 4-5%, driven by increasing ad monetization (CPC auction density)
extractions.evidence: Analyst projections suggest Pinduoduo main app could achieve RMB 4.7T GMV in 2025 (approximately 60% of Alibaba) with an improving take rate approaching 5%, matching Alibaba's level. The improvement is driven by: (1) higher CPC ad prices as merchant competition intensifies, (2) introduction of new ad formats, (3) deeper merchant dependency on Pinduoduo's traffic.

verification.verifier_notes: Based on analyst estimates. PDD has not disclosed GMV since 2020. Alibaba take rate ~5%, rising with Quanzhantui tool.

extractions.claim: Peer take rate comparison: PDD ~4-5%, Alibaba ~5% (rising), JD.com ~3-5% (1P margins), Shopee ~11-12% (includes logistics)
extractions.evidence: Take rate benchmarks across Asian e-commerce: Alibaba Taobao/Tmall: ~5%, increasing via 0.6% software service fee + Quanzhantui penetration (now 30%, target 45-50% in FY2026). JD.com: 3-5% effective margin on 1P retail, expanding 3P marketplace. Shopee: ~11-12% (revenue/GMV = $9B/$78.5B), includes logistics and payment fees. PDD: ~4-5% blended, but Pinduoduo domestic likely lower and Temu distorted by gross recognition.

verification.verifier_notes: Shopee take rate computed from FY2023 revenue $9B / GMV $78.5B = 11.5%. Alibaba from earnings calls. JD from 1P margin analysis.
